title: AD CS Relay / ESC8 Flow
description: This runbook operationalizes AD CS Relay / ESC8 Flow using #certipy , #ntlmrelayx.py . It is designed for repeatable, high-signal execution in authorized OSCP/CPTS-style assessments. Relays NTLM auth to AD CS enrollment endpoints to request privileged certificates.

metadata.descriptionHtml:
This runbook operationalizes AD CS Relay / ESC8 Flow using #certipy , #ntlmrelayx.py. It is designed for repeatable, high-signal execution in authorized OSCP/CPTS-style assessments.

Relays NTLM auth to AD CS enrollment endpoints to request privileged certificates.

  1. Prepare scope and access: validate target reachability and select low-noise execution order.
  2. Execute core workflow: run the command chain to produce actionable security outcomes.
  3. Validate and operationalize output: confirm results, capture artifacts, and prepare follow-on actions.
  • Tool Location/Source: primary references are in local source corpus entries linked from runbook-list.md; e.g., docs/sources-runbooks/oscp-cpts/p0/hackmd-oscp-pen200.md:373; docs/sources-runbooks/oscp-cpts/p0/0x1ceKing-HTB-Certified-Penetration-Testing-Specialist/cheatsheet/Active Directory Enumeration and Attacks.md:274.
  • Attack pre-requisite: authorized scope, operator shell access, and protocol reachability on TCP 445, TCP 80, TCP 443.
  • What attacker gain: validated findings (access paths, credentials, execution context, or exposure evidence) enabling faster compromise progression.
  • Detection/Defense opsec: monitor abnormal auth/enumeration bursts, enforce least privilege and hardening baselines, and alert on repeated tool-pattern telemetry.

Core Commands:

certipy relay -ca <CA_NAME> -target http://<CA_HOST>/certsrv/
ntlmrelayx.py -debug -smb2support --target http://<CA_HOST>/certsrv/certfnsh.asp --adcs --template DomainController
metadata.commandsBlock:
certipy relay -ca <CA_NAME> -target http://<CA_HOST>/certsrv/
ntlmrelayx.py -debug -smb2support --target http://<CA_HOST>/certsrv/certfnsh.asp --adcs --template DomainController

steps.tooltip: Early validation reduces noise and prevents invalid follow-on actions against unreachable or mismatched targets.

steps.name: Primary Execution
steps.description: Main command path executes against the target service or domain component.
steps.tooltip: The technique leverages service behavior, protocol assumptions, or misconfiguration to extract actionable state.
